{{Short description| part_of of Kofun period Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shibayama Kofun cluster | native_name =芝山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Tonozuka.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tonozuka Kofun in the Shibayama kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Chiba Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shibayama kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|51|N|140|25|04|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Yokoshibahikari, Chiba]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Shibayama Kofun cluster'''|芝山古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in what is now the Nakadai neighborhood of the town of [[Yokoshibahikari, Chiba|Yokoshibahikari]], [[Chiba Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1958.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138187|title=芝山古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> Despite its name, it is not located in the town of [[Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama]], but in neighboring Yokoshibahikari, and it is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nakadai kofun cluster'''|中台古墳群}}.
==Overview== The Shibayama site is located on the plateau on the east bank of the Kido River, which flows through the central Kujukuri Plain in north-central [[Bōsō Peninsula]]. The cluster consists of two keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' and 13 dome-shaped ''kofun''. The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1956 by [[Waseda University]], and is noteworthy for a unique group of ''[[haniwa]]'' depicting a complete a funeral procession.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Many of they artifacts discovered are stored and displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shibayama Haniwa Museum'''|芝山はにわ博物館|Shibayama Haniwa Hakubutsukan}}, located in the grounds of the temple of Kannonkyō-ji, in the town of Shibayama.
===Tonozuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Tonozuka'''|殿塚}} is the larger of the two tumuli, and is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 88 meters and was constructed in two tiers. The opening of the tumulus is orientated to the west and the tumulus was surrounded by a double rectangular moat. It was covered in ''haniwa'', many of which had been preserved by having fallen into the soft mud of the moat. In addition to many cylindrical ''haniwa'', the north side of the moat contained ''haniwa'' shaped as horses, dogs, cows, female deer, wild boar, birds (ducks and other waterfowl). ''Haniwa'' depicting men and women were found from the area of the posterior circular portion, and morning-glory shaped ''haniwa'' and two anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' were found on the north side. The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] was painted red on the interior. [[Grave goods]] included beads such as ''magatama'', gold rings, iron swords, gold bells, and bronze ornamental horse fittings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 88 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 58 meters wide x 10 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 58 meter diameter x 10.4 meters high, 2-tiers
===Himezuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Himezuka'''|姫塚}} is the smaller of the two [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]], and has a total length of 58.8 meters. It was constructed in parallel to, and 30 meters to the north, of the Tonozuka. An excavation in the year 2000 found that it also had a double moat. The horizontal stone-lined burial chamber has a total length of 5.72 meters. As with the Tonozuka, grave goods included ''magatama'', gold rings, bronze ornaments, a large-sized iron sword, iron dagger, gold-copper decorative horse fittings, etc. The ''haniwa'' at the Himezuka were found to be in situ and were in excellent preservation. Of especial interest was a depiction of people and horses in a funeral procession extending for 50 meters in a line from the front of the tumulus to the back of the posterior circular portion, which contained the opening to the burial chamber. This procession included a group of men on horses wearing hats, and four horses with sandals, five warriors, followed by a group of 16 men with a wheel-shaped ''haniwa'', a group of seven women, followed by ten more men. Among these figures were a warrior with a beard, a farmer with a hoe, and a man kneeling with a musical instrument. These ''haniwa'' were all dated to the latter half of the 6th century AD and represent the peak of ''haniwa'' expression.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 58.8 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 35 meters wide x 4.5 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 35 meter diameter x 5 meters high
===Other tumuli=== The other tumuli in the cluster are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with diameters of around 20 meters. They are well preserved and remain unexcavated.
The site is located a five-minute walk from the Yokoshiba Nakadai bus stop on the airport shuttle bus from [[Keisei Narita Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/n411-019.html Chiba Prefecture official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/0000001838.html Yokoshibahikari town official home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.haniwakan.com Shibayama Haniwa Museum] {{in lang|ja}}

===Tonozuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Tonozuka'''|殿塚}} is the larger of the two tumuli, and is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 88 meters and was constructed in two tiers. The opening of the tumulus is orientated to the west and the tumulus was surrounded by a double rectangular moat. It was covered in ''haniwa'', many of which had been preserved by having fallen into the soft mud of the moat. In addition to many cylindrical ''haniwa'', the north side of the moat contained ''haniwa'' shaped as horses, dogs, cows, female deer, wild boar, birds (ducks and other waterfowl). ''Haniwa'' depicting men and women were found from the area of the posterior circular portion, and morning-glory shaped ''haniwa'' and two anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' were found on the north side. The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] was painted red on the interior. [[Grave goods]] included beads such as ''magatama'', gold rings, iron swords, gold bells, and bronze ornamental horse fittings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 88 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 58 meters wide x 10 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 58 meter diameter x 10.4 meters high, 2-tiers
===Himezuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Himezuka'''|姫塚}} is the smaller of the two [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]], and has a total length of 58.8 meters. It was constructed in parallel to, and 30 meters to the north, of the Tonozuka. An excavation in the year 2000 found that it also had a double moat. The horizontal stone-lined burial chamber has a total length of 5.72 meters. As with the Tonozuka, grave goods included ''magatama'', gold rings, bronze ornaments, a large-sized iron sword, iron dagger, gold-copper decorative horse fittings, etc. The ''haniwa'' at the Himezuka were found to be in situ and were in excellent preservation. Of especial interest was a depiction of people and horses in a funeral procession extending for 50 meters in a line from the front of the tumulus to the back of the posterior circular portion, which contained the opening to the burial chamber. This procession included a group of men on horses wearing hats, and four horses with sandals, five warriors, followed by a group of 16 men with a wheel-shaped ''haniwa'', a group of seven women, followed by ten more men. Among these figures were a warrior with a beard, a farmer with a hoe, and a man kneeling with a musical instrument. These ''haniwa'' were all dated to the latter half of the 6th century AD and represent the peak of ''haniwa'' expression.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 58.8 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 35 meters wide x 4.5 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 35 meter diameter x 5 meters high
===Other tumuli=== The other tumuli in the cluster are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with diameters of around 20 meters. They are well preserved and remain unexcavated.
The site is located a five-minute walk from the Yokoshiba Nakadai bus stop on the airport shuttle bus from [[Keisei Narita Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Shibayama Kofun cluster'''|芝山古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in what is now the Nakadai neighborhood of the town of [[Yokoshibahikari, Chiba|Yokoshibahikari]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1958.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138187|title=芝山古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> Despite its name, it is not located in the town of [[Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama]], but in neighboring Yokoshibahikari, and it is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nakadai kofun cluster'''|中台古墳群}}.
==Overview== The Shibayama site is located on the plateau on the east bank of the Kido River, which flows through the central Kujukuri Plain in north-central [[Bōsō Peninsula]]. The cluster consists of two keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' and 13 dome-shaped ''kofun''. ===Tonozuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Tonozuka'''|殿塚}} is the larger of the two tumuli, and is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 88 meters and was constructed in two tiers. The opening of the tumulus is orientated to the west and the tumulus was surrounded by a double rectangular moat. It was covered in ''haniwa'', many of which had been preserved by having fallen into the soft mud of the moat. In addition to many cylindrical ''haniwa'', the north side of the moat contained ''haniwa'' shaped as horses, dogs, cows, female deer, wild boar, birds (ducks and other waterfowl). ''Haniwa'' depicting men and women were found from the area of the posterior circular portion, and morning-glory shaped ''haniwa'' and two anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' were found on the north side. The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] was painted red on the interior. [[Grave goods]] included beads such as ''magatama'', gold rings, iron swords, gold bells, and bronze ornamental horse fittings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 88 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 58 meters wide x 10 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 58 meter diameter x 10.4 meters high, 2-tiers
===Himezuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Himezuka'''|姫塚}} is the smaller of the two [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]], and has a total length of 58.8 meters. It was constructed in parallel to, and 30 meters to the north, of the Tonozuka. An excavation in the year 2000 found that it also had a double moat. The horizontal stone-lined burial chamber has a total length of 5.72 meters. As with the Tonozuka, grave goods included ''magatama'', gold rings, bronze ornaments, a large-sized iron sword, iron dagger, gold-copper decorative horse fittings, etc. The ''haniwa'' at the Himezuka were found to be in situ and were in excellent preservation. Of especial interest was a depiction of people and horses in a funeral procession extending for 50 meters in a line from the front of the tumulus to the back of the posterior circular portion, which contained the opening to the burial chamber. This procession included a group of men on horses wearing hats, and four horses with sandals, five warriors, followed by a group of 16 men with a wheel-shaped ''haniwa'', a group of seven women, followed by ten more men. Among these figures were a warrior with a beard, a farmer with a hoe, and a man kneeling with a musical instrument. These ''haniwa'' were all dated to the latter half of the 6th century AD and represent the peak of ''haniwa'' expression.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 58.8 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 35 meters wide x 4.5 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 35 meter diameter x 5 meters high
===Other tumuli=== The other tumuli in the cluster are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with diameters of around 20 meters. They are well preserved and remain unexcavated.
The site is located a five-minute walk from the Yokoshiba Nakadai bus stop on the airport shuttle bus from [[Keisei Narita Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

===Other tumuli=== The other tumuli in the cluster are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with diameters of around 20 meters. They are well preserved and remain unexcavated.
The site is located a five-minute walk from the Yokoshiba Nakadai bus stop on the airport shuttle bus from [[Keisei Narita Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Short description| part_of of Kofun period Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shibayama Kofun cluster | native_name =芝山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Tonozuka.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tonozuka Kofun in the Shibayama kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Chiba Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shibayama kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|51|N|140|25|04|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Yokoshibahikari, Chiba]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Shibayama Kofun cluster'''|芝山古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in what is now the Nakadai neighborhood of the town of [[Yokoshibahikari, Chiba|Yokoshibahikari]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1958.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138187|title=芝山古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> Despite its name, it is not located in the town of [[Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama]], but in neighboring Yokoshibahikari, and it is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nakadai kofun cluster'''|中台古墳群}}.
==Overview== The Shibayama site is located on the plateau on the east bank of the Kido River, which flows through the central Kujukuri Plain in north-central [[:en:Bōsō Peninsula|Bōsō Peninsula]]. The cluster consists of two keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' and 13 dome-shaped ''kofun''. The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1956 by [[Waseda University]], and is noteworthy for a unique group of ''[[haniwa]]'' depicting a complete a funeral procession.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Many of they artifacts discovered are stored and displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shibayama Haniwa Museum'''|芝山はにわ博物館|Shibayama Haniwa Hakubutsukan}}, located in the grounds of the temple of Kannonkyō-ji, in the town of Shibayama.
===Tonozuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Tonozuka'''|殿塚}} is the larger of the two tumuli, and is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 88 meters and was constructed in two tiers. The opening of the tumulus is orientated to the west and the tumulus was surrounded by a double rectangular moat. It was covered in ''haniwa'', many of which had been preserved by having fallen into the soft mud of the moat. In addition to many cylindrical ''haniwa'', the north side of the moat contained ''haniwa'' shaped as horses, dogs, cows, female deer, wild boar, birds (ducks and other waterfowl). ''Haniwa'' depicting men and women were found from the area of the posterior circular portion, and morning-glory shaped ''haniwa'' and two anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' were found on the north side. The horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was painted red on the interior. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included beads such as ''magatama'', gold rings, iron swords, gold bells, and bronze ornamental horse fittings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 88 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 58 meters wide x 10 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 58 meter diameter x 10.4 meters high, 2-tiers
===Himezuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Himezuka'''|姫塚}} is the smaller of the two [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]], and has a total length of 58.8 meters. It was constructed in parallel to, and 30 meters to the north, of the Tonozuka. An excavation in the year 2000 found that it also had a double moat. The horizontal stone-lined burial chamber has a total length of 5.72 meters. As with the Tonozuka, grave goods included ''magatama'', gold rings, bronze ornaments, a large-sized iron sword, iron dagger, gold-copper decorative horse fittings, etc. The ''haniwa'' at the Himezuka were found to be in situ and were in excellent preservation. Of especial interest was a depiction of people and horses in a funeral procession extending for 50 meters in a line from the front of the tumulus to the back of the posterior circular portion, which contained the opening to the burial chamber. This procession included a group of men on horses wearing hats, and four horses with sandals, five warriors, followed by a group of 16 men with a wheel-shaped ''haniwa'', a group of seven women, followed by ten more men. Among these figures were a warrior with a beard, a farmer with a hoe, and a man kneeling with a musical instrument. These ''haniwa'' were all dated to the latter half of the 6th century AD and represent the peak of ''haniwa'' expression.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 58.8 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 35 meters wide x 4.5 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 35 meter diameter x 5 meters high
===Other tumuli=== The other tumuli in the cluster are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with diameters of around 20 meters. They are well preserved and remain unexcavated.
The site is located a five-minute walk from the Yokoshiba Nakadai bus stop on the airport shuttle bus from [[Keisei Narita Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/n411-019.html Chiba Prefecture official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/0000001838.html Yokoshibahikari town official home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.haniwakan.com Shibayama Haniwa Museum] {{in lang|ja}}

[[Category:Completion tier 4]]

The {{nihongo|'''Shibayama Kofun cluster'''|芝山古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in what is now the Nakadai neighborhood of the town of [[Yokoshibahikari, Chiba|Yokoshibahikari]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1958.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138187|title=芝山古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> Despite its name, it is not located in the town of [[Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama]], but in neighboring Yokoshibahikari, and it is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nakadai kofun cluster'''|中台古墳群}}.

===Tonozuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Tonozuka'''|殿塚}} is the larger of the two tumuli, and is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 88 meters and was constructed in two tiers. The opening of the tumulus is orientated to the west and the tumulus was surrounded by a double rectangular moat. It was covered in ''haniwa'', many of which had been preserved by having fallen into the soft mud of the moat. In addition to many cylindrical ''haniwa'', the north side of the moat contained ''haniwa'' shaped as horses, dogs, cows, female deer, wild boar, birds (ducks and other waterfowl). ''Haniwa'' depicting men and women were found from the area of the posterior circular portion, and morning-glory shaped ''haniwa'' and two anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' were found on the north side. The horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was painted red on the interior. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included beads such as ''magatama'', gold rings, iron swords, gold bells, and bronze ornamental horse fittings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 88 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 58 meters wide x 10 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 58 meter diameter x 10.4 meters high, 2-tiers
===Himezuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Himezuka'''|姫塚}} is the smaller of the two [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]], and has a total length of 58.8 meters. It was constructed in parallel to, and 30 meters to the north, of the Tonozuka. An excavation in the year 2000 found that it also had a double moat. These ''haniwa'' were all dated to the latter half of the 6th century AD and represent the peak of ''haniwa'' expression.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Shibayama Kofun cluster'''|芝山古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in what is now the Nakadai neighborhood of the town of [[Yokoshibahikari, Chiba|Yokoshibahikari]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1958.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138187|title=芝山古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> Despite its name, it is not located in the town of [[Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama]], but in neighboring Yokoshibahikari, and it is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nakadai kofun cluster'''|中台古墳群}}.
==Overview== The Shibayama site is located on the plateau on the east bank of the Kido River, which flows through the central Kujukuri Plain in north-central [[:en:Bōsō Peninsula|Bōsō Peninsula]]. The cluster consists of two keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' and 13 dome-shaped ''kofun''. The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1956 by [[Waseda University]], and is noteworthy for a unique group of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' depicting a complete a funeral procession.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Many of they artifacts discovered are stored and displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shibayama Haniwa Museum'''|芝山はにわ博物館|Shibayama Haniwa Hakubutsukan}}, located in the grounds of the temple of Kannonkyō-ji, in the town of Shibayama.
===Tonozuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Tonozuka'''|殿塚}} is the larger of the two tumuli, and is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 88 meters and was constructed in two tiers. The opening of the tumulus is orientated to the west and the tumulus was surrounded by a double rectangular moat. It was covered in ''haniwa'', many of which had been preserved by having fallen into the soft mud of the moat. In addition to many cylindrical ''haniwa'', the north side of the moat contained ''haniwa'' shaped as horses, dogs, cows, female deer, wild boar, birds (ducks and other waterfowl). ''Haniwa'' depicting men and women were found from the area of the posterior circular portion, and morning-glory shaped ''haniwa'' and two anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' were found on the north side. The horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was painted red on the interior. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included beads such as ''magatama'', gold rings, iron swords, gold bells, and bronze ornamental horse fittings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 88 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 58 meters wide x 10 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 58 meter diameter x 10.4 meters high, 2-tiers
===Himezuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Himezuka'''|姫塚}} is the smaller of the two [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]], and has a total length of 58.8 meters. It was constructed in parallel to, and 30 meters to the north, of the Tonozuka. An excavation in the year 2000 found that it also had a double moat. The horizontal stone-lined burial chamber has a total length of 5.72 meters. As with the Tonozuka, grave goods included ''magatama'', gold rings, bronze ornaments, a large-sized iron sword, iron dagger, gold-copper decorative horse fittings, etc. The ''haniwa'' at the Himezuka were found to be in situ and were in excellent preservation. Of especial interest was a depiction of people and horses in a funeral procession extending for 50 meters in a line from the front of the tumulus to the back of the posterior circular portion, which contained the opening to the burial chamber. This procession included a group of men on horses wearing hats, and four horses with sandals, five warriors, followed by a group of 16 men with a wheel-shaped ''haniwa'', a group of seven women, followed by ten more men. Among these figures were a warrior with a beard, a farmer with a hoe, and a man kneeling with a musical instrument. These ''haniwa'' were all dated to the latter half of the 6th century AD and represent the peak of ''haniwa'' expression.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 58.8 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 35 meters wide x 4.5 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 35 meter diameter x 5 meters high
===Other tumuli=== The other tumuli in the cluster are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with diameters of around 20 meters. They are well preserved and remain unexcavated.
The site is located a five-minute walk from the Yokoshiba Nakadai bus stop on the airport shuttle bus from [[Keisei Narita Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/n411-019.html Chiba Prefecture official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/0000001838.html Yokoshibahikari town official home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.haniwakan.com Shibayama Haniwa Museum] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

[[Category:History of Chiba Prefecture]]
[[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
[[Category:Completion tier 4]]
[[sn:Shibayama Kofun Cluster]]}}

==Overview== The Shibayama site is located on the plateau on the east bank of the Kido River, which flows through the central Kujukuri Plain in north-central [[:en:Bōsō Peninsula|Bōsō Peninsula]]. The cluster consists of two keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' and 13 dome-shaped ''kofun''. The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1956 by [[Waseda University]], and is noteworthy for a unique group of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' depicting a complete a funeral procession.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Many of they artifacts discovered are stored and displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shibayama Haniwa Museum'''|芝山はにわ博物館|Shibayama Haniwa Hakubutsukan}}, located in the grounds of the temple of Kannonkyō-ji, in the town of Shibayama.
===Tonozuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Tonozuka'''|殿塚}} is the larger of the two tumuli, and is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 88 meters and was constructed in two tiers. The opening of the tumulus is orientated to the west and the tumulus was surrounded by a double rectangular moat. It was covered in ''haniwa'', many of which had been preserved by having fallen into the soft mud of the moat. In addition to many cylindrical ''haniwa'', the north side of the moat contained ''haniwa'' shaped as horses, dogs, cows, female deer, wild boar, birds (ducks and other waterfowl). ''Haniwa'' depicting men and women were found from the area of the posterior circular portion, and morning-glory shaped ''haniwa'' and two anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' were found on the north side. The horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was painted red on the interior. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included beads such as ''magatama'', gold rings, iron swords, gold bells, and bronze ornamental horse fittings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 88 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 58 meters wide x 10 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 58 meter diameter x 10.4 meters high, 2-tiers
===Himezuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Himezuka'''|姫塚}} is the smaller of the two [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]], and has a total length of 58.8 meters. It was constructed in parallel to, and 30 meters to the north, of the Tonozuka. An excavation in the year 2000 found that it also had a double moat. The horizontal stone-lined burial chamber has a total length of 5.72 meters. As with the Tonozuka, grave goods included ''magatama'', gold rings, bronze ornaments, a large-sized iron sword, iron dagger, gold-copper decorative horse fittings, etc. The ''haniwa'' at the Himezuka were found to be in situ and were in excellent preservation. Of especial interest was a depiction of people and horses in a funeral procession extending for 50 meters in a line from the front of the tumulus to the back of the posterior circular portion, which contained the opening to the burial chamber. This procession included a group of men on horses wearing hats, and four horses with sandals, five warriors, followed by a group of 16 men with a wheel-shaped ''haniwa'', a group of seven women, followed by ten more men. Among these figures were a warrior with a beard, a farmer with a hoe, and a man kneeling with a musical instrument. These ''haniwa'' were all dated to the latter half of the 6th century AD and represent the peak of ''haniwa'' expression.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 58.8 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 35 meters wide x 4.5 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 35 meter diameter x 5 meters high
===Other tumuli=== The other tumuli in the cluster are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with diameters of around 20 meters. They are well preserved and remain unexcavated.
The site is located a five-minute walk from the Yokoshiba Nakadai bus stop on the airport shuttle bus from [[:en:Keisei Narita Station|Keisei Narita Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]]

==External links== *[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/n411-019.html Chiba Prefecture official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/0000001838.html Yokoshibahikari town official home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.haniwakan.com Shibayama Haniwa Museum] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Shibayama Kofun cluster'''|芝山古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in what is now the Nakadai neighborhood of the town of [[Yokoshibahikari, Chiba|Yokoshibahikari]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1958.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138187|title=芝山古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> Despite its name, it is not located in the town of [[Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama]], but in neighboring Yokoshibahikari, and it is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nakadai kofun cluster'''|中台古墳群}}.
==Overview== The Shibayama site is located on the plateau on the east bank of the Kido River, which flows through the central Kujukuri Plain in north-central [[:en:Bōsō Peninsula|Bōsō Peninsula]]. The cluster consists of two keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' and 13 dome-shaped ''kofun''. The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1956 by [[:en:Waseda University|Waseda University]], and is noteworthy for a unique group of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' depicting a complete a funeral procession.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Many of they artifacts discovered are stored and displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shibayama Haniwa Museum'''|芝山はにわ博物館|Shibayama Haniwa Hakubutsukan}}, located in the grounds of the temple of Kannonkyō-ji, in the town of Shibayama.
===Tonozuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Tonozuka'''|殿塚}} is the larger of the two tumuli, and is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 88 meters and was constructed in two tiers. The opening of the tumulus is orientated to the west and the tumulus was surrounded by a double rectangular moat. It was covered in ''haniwa'', many of which had been preserved by having fallen into the soft mud of the moat. In addition to many cylindrical ''haniwa'', the north side of the moat contained ''haniwa'' shaped as horses, dogs, cows, female deer, wild boar, birds (ducks and other waterfowl). ''Haniwa'' depicting men and women were found from the area of the posterior circular portion, and morning-glory shaped ''haniwa'' and two anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' were found on the north side. The horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was painted red on the interior. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included beads such as ''magatama'', gold rings, iron swords, gold bells, and bronze ornamental horse fittings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 88 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 58 meters wide x 10 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 58 meter diameter x 10.4 meters high, 2-tiers
===Himezuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Himezuka'''|姫塚}} is the smaller of the two [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]], and has a total length of 58.8 meters. It was constructed in parallel to, and 30 meters to the north, of the Tonozuka. An excavation in the year 2000 found that it also had a double moat. The horizontal stone-lined burial chamber has a total length of 5.72 meters. As with the Tonozuka, grave goods included ''magatama'', gold rings, bronze ornaments, a large-sized iron sword, iron dagger, gold-copper decorative horse fittings, etc. The ''haniwa'' at the Himezuka were found to be in situ and were in excellent preservation. Of especial interest was a depiction of people and horses in a funeral procession extending for 50 meters in a line from the front of the tumulus to the back of the posterior circular portion, which contained the opening to the burial chamber. This procession included a group of men on horses wearing hats, and four horses with sandals, five warriors, followed by a group of 16 men with a wheel-shaped ''haniwa'', a group of seven women, followed by ten more men. Among these figures were a warrior with a beard, a farmer with a hoe, and a man kneeling with a musical instrument. These ''haniwa'' were all dated to the latter half of the 6th century AD and represent the peak of ''haniwa'' expression.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 58.8 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 35 meters wide x 4.5 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 35 meter diameter x 5 meters high
===Other tumuli=== The other tumuli in the cluster are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with diameters of around 20 meters. They are well preserved and remain unexcavated.
The site is located a five-minute walk from the Yokoshiba Nakadai bus stop on the airport shuttle bus from [[:en:Keisei Narita Station|Keisei Narita Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

===Himezuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Himezuka'''|姫塚}} is the smaller of the two [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]], and has a total length of 58.8 meters. It was constructed in parallel to, and 30 meters to the north, of the Tonozuka. An excavation in the year 2000 found that it also had a double moat. The horizontal stone-lined burial chamber has a total length of 5.72 meters. As with the Tonozuka, grave goods included ''magatama'', gold rings, bronze ornaments, a large-sized iron sword, iron dagger, gold-copper decorative horse fittings, etc. The ''haniwa'' at the Himezuka were found to be in situ and were in excellent preservation. Of especial interest was a depiction of people and horses in a funeral procession extending for 50 meters in a line from the front of the tumulus to the back of the posterior circular portion, which contained the opening to the burial chamber. This procession included a group of men on horses wearing hats, and four horses with sandals, five warriors, followed by a group of 16 men with a wheel-shaped ''haniwa'', a group of seven women, followed by ten more men. Among these figures were a warrior with a beard, a farmer with a hoe, and a man kneeling with a musical instrument. These ''haniwa'' were all dated to the latter half of the 6th century AD and represent the peak of ''haniwa'' expression.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 58.8 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 35 meters wide x 4.5 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 35 meter diameter x 5 meters high
===Other tumuli=== The other tumuli in the cluster are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with diameters of around 20 meters. They are well preserved and remain unexcavated.
The site is located a five-minute walk from the Yokoshiba Nakadai bus stop on the airport shuttle bus from [[:en:Keisei Narita Station|Keisei Narita Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description| part_of of Kofun period Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shibayama Kofun cluster | native_name =芝山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Tonozuka.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tonozuka Kofun in the Shibayama kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Chiba Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shibayama kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|51|N|140|25|04|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Yokoshibahikari, Chiba|Yokoshibahikari, Chiba]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Shibayama Kofun cluster'''|芝山古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in what is now the Nakadai neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yokoshibahikari, Chiba|Yokoshibahikari]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1958.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138187|title=芝山古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> Despite its name, it is not located in the town of [[Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama]], but in neighboring Yokoshibahikari, and it is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nakadai kofun cluster'''|中台古墳群}}.
==Overview== The Shibayama site is located on the plateau on the east bank of the Kido River, which flows through the central Kujukuri Plain in north-central [[:en:Bōsō Peninsula|Bōsō Peninsula]]. The cluster consists of two keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' and 13 dome-shaped ''kofun''. The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1956 by [[:en:Waseda University|Waseda University]], and is noteworthy for a unique group of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' depicting a complete a funeral procession.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Many of they artifacts discovered are stored and displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shibayama Haniwa Museum'''|芝山はにわ博物館|Shibayama Haniwa Hakubutsukan}}, located in the grounds of the temple of Kannonkyō-ji, in the town of Shibayama.
===Tonozuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Tonozuka'''|殿塚}} is the larger of the two tumuli, and is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 88 meters and was constructed in two tiers. The opening of the tumulus is orientated to the west and the tumulus was surrounded by a double rectangular moat. It was covered in ''haniwa'', many of which had been preserved by having fallen into the soft mud of the moat. In addition to many cylindrical ''haniwa'', the north side of the moat contained ''haniwa'' shaped as horses, dogs, cows, female deer, wild boar, birds (ducks and other waterfowl). ''Haniwa'' depicting men and women were found from the area of the posterior circular portion, and morning-glory shaped ''haniwa'' and two anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' were found on the north side. The horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was painted red on the interior. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included beads such as ''magatama'', gold rings, iron swords, gold bells, and bronze ornamental horse fittings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 88 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 58 meters wide x 10 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 58 meter diameter x 10.4 meters high, 2-tiers
===Himezuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Himezuka'''|姫塚}} is the smaller of the two [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]], and has a total length of 58.8 meters. It was constructed in parallel to, and 30 meters to the north, of the Tonozuka. An excavation in the year 2000 found that it also had a double moat. The horizontal stone-lined burial chamber has a total length of 5.72 meters. As with the Tonozuka, grave goods included ''magatama'', gold rings, bronze ornaments, a large-sized iron sword, iron dagger, gold-copper decorative horse fittings, etc. The ''haniwa'' at the Himezuka were found to be in situ and were in excellent preservation. Of especial interest was a depiction of people and horses in a funeral procession extending for 50 meters in a line from the front of the tumulus to the back of the posterior circular portion, which contained the opening to the burial chamber. This procession included a group of men on horses wearing hats, and four horses with sandals, five warriors, followed by a group of 16 men with a wheel-shaped ''haniwa'', a group of seven women, followed by ten more men. Among these figures were a warrior with a beard, a farmer with a hoe, and a man kneeling with a musical instrument. These ''haniwa'' were all dated to the latter half of the 6th century AD and represent the peak of ''haniwa'' expression.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 58.8 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 35 meters wide x 4.5 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 35 meter diameter x 5 meters high
===Other tumuli=== The other tumuli in the cluster are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with diameters of around 20 meters. They are well preserved and remain unexcavated.
The site is located a five-minute walk from the Yokoshiba Nakadai bus stop on the airport shuttle bus from [[:en:Keisei Narita Station|Keisei Narita Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11614635]] Wikidata name: Shibayama Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description| part_of of Kofun period Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shibayama Kofun cluster | native_name =芝山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Tonozuka.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tonozuka Kofun in the Shibayama kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Chiba Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shibayama kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|51|N|140|25|04|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Yokoshibahikari, Chiba|Yokoshibahikari, Chiba]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Shibayama Kofun cluster'''|芝山古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in what is now the Nakadai neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yokoshibahikari, Chiba|Yokoshibahikari]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1958.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138187|title=芝山古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> Despite its name, it is not located in the town of [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama]], but in neighboring Yokoshibahikari, and it is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nakadai kofun cluster'''|中台古墳群}}.
==Overview== The Shibayama site is located on the plateau on the east bank of the Kido River, which flows through the central Kujukuri Plain in north-central [[:en:Bōsō Peninsula|Bōsō Peninsula]]. The cluster consists of two keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' and 13 dome-shaped ''kofun''. The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1956 by [[:en:Waseda University|Waseda University]], and is noteworthy for a unique group of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' depicting a complete a funeral procession.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Many of they artifacts discovered are stored and displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shibayama Haniwa Museum'''|芝山はにわ博物館|Shibayama Haniwa Hakubutsukan}}, located in the grounds of the temple of Kannonkyō-ji, in the town of Shibayama.
===Tonozuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Tonozuka'''|殿塚}} is the larger of the two tumuli, and is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 88 meters and was constructed in two tiers. The opening of the tumulus is orientated to the west and the tumulus was surrounded by a double rectangular moat. It was covered in ''haniwa'', many of which had been preserved by having fallen into the soft mud of the moat. In addition to many cylindrical ''haniwa'', the north side of the moat contained ''haniwa'' shaped as horses, dogs, cows, female deer, wild boar, birds (ducks and other waterfowl). ''Haniwa'' depicting men and women were found from the area of the posterior circular portion, and morning-glory shaped ''haniwa'' and two anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' were found on the north side. The horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was painted red on the interior. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included beads such as ''magatama'', gold rings, iron swords, gold bells, and bronze ornamental horse fittings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 88 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 58 meters wide x 10 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 58 meter diameter x 10.4 meters high, 2-tiers
===Himezuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Himezuka'''|姫塚}} is the smaller of the two [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]], and has a total length of 58.8 meters. It was constructed in parallel to, and 30 meters to the north, of the Tonozuka. An excavation in the year 2000 found that it also had a double moat. The horizontal stone-lined burial chamber has a total length of 5.72 meters. As with the Tonozuka, grave goods included ''magatama'', gold rings, bronze ornaments, a large-sized iron sword, iron dagger, gold-copper decorative horse fittings, etc. The ''haniwa'' at the Himezuka were found to be in situ and were in excellent preservation. Of especial interest was a depiction of people and horses in a funeral procession extending for 50 meters in a line from the front of the tumulus to the back of the posterior circular portion, which contained the opening to the burial chamber. This procession included a group of men on horses wearing hats, and four horses with sandals, five warriors, followed by a group of 16 men with a wheel-shaped ''haniwa'', a group of seven women, followed by ten more men. Among these figures were a warrior with a beard, a farmer with a hoe, and a man kneeling with a musical instrument. These ''haniwa'' were all dated to the latter half of the 6th century AD and represent the peak of ''haniwa'' expression.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 58.8 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 35 meters wide x 4.5 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 35 meter diameter x 5 meters high
===Other tumuli=== The other tumuli in the cluster are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with diameters of around 20 meters. They are well preserved and remain unexcavated.
The site is located a five-minute walk from the Yokoshiba Nakadai bus stop on the airport shuttle bus from [[:en:Keisei Narita Station|Keisei Narita Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/n411-019.html Chiba Prefecture official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/0000001838.html Yokoshibahikari town official home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.haniwakan.com Shibayama Haniwa Museum] {{in lang|ja}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11614635]] Wikidata name: Shibayama Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description| part_of of Kofun period Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shibayama Kofun cluster | native_name =芝山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Tonozuka.JPG|image= File:Tonozuka.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = Tonozuka Kofun in the Shibayama kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Chiba Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shibayama kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|40|51|N|140|25|04|E|display=inline}}; {{Coord|35.680833|N|140.419722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Yokoshibahikari, Chiba|Yokoshibahikari, Chiba]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|qid=Q1203260}}| region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =; 0600| abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Museum) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles =; {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718}}| architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Shibayama Kofun cluster'''|芝山古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in what is now the Nakadai neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yokoshibahikari, Chiba|Yokoshibahikari]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1958.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138187|title=芝山古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> Despite its name, it is not located in the town of [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama]], but in neighboring Yokoshibahikari, and it is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nakadai kofun cluster'''|中台古墳群}}.
==Overview== The Shibayama site is located on the plateau on the east bank of the Kido River, which flows through the central Kujukuri Plain in north-central [[:en:Bōsō Peninsula|Bōsō Peninsula]]. The cluster consists of two keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' and 13 dome-shaped ''kofun''. The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1956 by [[:en:Waseda University|Waseda University]], and is noteworthy for a unique group of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' depicting a complete a funeral procession.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Many of they artifacts discovered are stored and displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shibayama Haniwa Museum'''|芝山はにわ博物館|Shibayama Haniwa Hakubutsukan}}, located in the grounds of the temple of Kannonkyō-ji, in the town of Shibayama.
===Tonozuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Tonozuka'''|殿塚}} is the larger of the two tumuli, and is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 88 meters and was constructed in two tiers. The opening of the tumulus is orientated to the west and the tumulus was surrounded by a double rectangular moat. It was covered in ''haniwa'', many of which had been preserved by having fallen into the soft mud of the moat. In addition to many cylindrical ''haniwa'', the north side of the moat contained ''haniwa'' shaped as horses, dogs, cows, female deer, wild boar, birds (ducks and other waterfowl). ''Haniwa'' depicting men and women were found from the area of the posterior circular portion, and morning-glory shaped ''haniwa'' and two anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' were found on the north side. The horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was painted red on the interior. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included beads such as ''magatama'', gold rings, iron swords, gold bells, and bronze ornamental horse fittings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 88 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 58 meters wide x 10 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 58 meter diameter x 10.4 meters high, 2-tiers
===Himezuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Himezuka'''|姫塚}} is the smaller of the two [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]], and has a total length of 58.8 meters. It was constructed in parallel to, and 30 meters to the north, of the Tonozuka. An excavation in the year 2000 found that it also had a double moat. The horizontal stone-lined burial chamber has a total length of 5.72 meters. As with the Tonozuka, grave goods included ''magatama'', gold rings, bronze ornaments, a large-sized iron sword, iron dagger, gold-copper decorative horse fittings, etc. The ''haniwa'' at the Himezuka were found to be in situ and were in excellent preservation. Of especial interest was a depiction of people and horses in a funeral procession extending for 50 meters in a line from the front of the tumulus to the back of the posterior circular portion, which contained the opening to the burial chamber. This procession included a group of men on horses wearing hats, and four horses with sandals, five warriors, followed by a group of 16 men with a wheel-shaped ''haniwa'', a group of seven women, followed by ten more men. Among these figures were a warrior with a beard, a farmer with a hoe, and a man kneeling with a musical instrument. These ''haniwa'' were all dated to the latter half of the 6th century AD and represent the peak of ''haniwa'' expression.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 58.8 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 35 meters wide x 4.5 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 35 meter diameter x 5 meters high
===Other tumuli=== The other tumuli in the cluster are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with diameters of around 20 meters. They are well preserved and remain unexcavated.
The site is located a five-minute walk from the Yokoshiba Nakadai bus stop on the airport shuttle bus from [[:en:Keisei Narita Station|Keisei Narita Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11614635]] Wikidata name: Shibayama Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description| part_of of Kofun period Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shibayama Kofun cluster | native_name =芝山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Tonozuka.JPG|image= File:Tonozuka.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = Tonozuka Kofun in the Shibayama kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Chiba Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shibayama kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|40|51|N|140|25|04|E|display=inline}}; {{Coord|35.680833|N|140.419722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Yokoshibahikari, Chiba|Yokoshibahikari, Chiba]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|qid=Q1203260}}| region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =; 0600| abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Museum) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles =; {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Shibayama Kofun cluster'''|芝山古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in what is now the Nakadai neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yokoshibahikari, Chiba|Yokoshibahikari]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1958.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138187|title=芝山古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> Despite its name, it is not located in the town of [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama]], but in neighboring Yokoshibahikari, and it is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nakadai kofun cluster'''|中台古墳群}}.
==Overview== The Shibayama site is located on the plateau on the east bank of the Kido River, which flows through the central Kujukuri Plain in north-central [[:en:Bōsō Peninsula|Bōsō Peninsula]]. The cluster consists of two keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' and 13 dome-shaped ''kofun''. The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1956 by [[:en:Waseda University|Waseda University]], and is noteworthy for a unique group of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' depicting a complete a funeral procession.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Many of they artifacts discovered are stored and displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shibayama Haniwa Museum'''|芝山はにわ博物館|Shibayama Haniwa Hakubutsukan}}, located in the grounds of the temple of Kannonkyō-ji, in the town of Shibayama.
===Tonozuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Tonozuka'''|殿塚}} is the larger of the two tumuli, and is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 88 meters and was constructed in two tiers. The opening of the tumulus is orientated to the west and the tumulus was surrounded by a double rectangular moat. It was covered in ''haniwa'', many of which had been preserved by having fallen into the soft mud of the moat. In addition to many cylindrical ''haniwa'', the north side of the moat contained ''haniwa'' shaped as horses, dogs, cows, female deer, wild boar, birds (ducks and other waterfowl). ''Haniwa'' depicting men and women were found from the area of the posterior circular portion, and morning-glory shaped ''haniwa'' and two anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' were found on the north side. The horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was painted red on the interior. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included beads such as ''magatama'', gold rings, iron swords, gold bells, and bronze ornamental horse fittings.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 88 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 58 meters wide x 10 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 58 meter diameter x 10.4 meters high, 2-tiers
===Himezuka=== The {{nihongo|'''Himezuka'''|姫塚}} is the smaller of the two [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]], and has a total length of 58.8 meters. It was constructed in parallel to, and 30 meters to the north, of the Tonozuka. An excavation in the year 2000 found that it also had a double moat. The horizontal stone-lined burial chamber has a total length of 5.72 meters. As with the Tonozuka, grave goods included ''magatama'', gold rings, bronze ornaments, a large-sized iron sword, iron dagger, gold-copper decorative horse fittings, etc. The ''haniwa'' at the Himezuka were found to be in situ and were in excellent preservation. Of especial interest was a depiction of people and horses in a funeral procession extending for 50 meters in a line from the front of the tumulus to the back of the posterior circular portion, which contained the opening to the burial chamber. This procession included a group of men on horses wearing hats, and four horses with sandals, five warriors, followed by a group of 16 men with a wheel-shaped ''haniwa'', a group of seven women, followed by ten more men. Among these figures were a warrior with a beard, a farmer with a hoe, and a man kneeling with a musical instrument. These ''haniwa'' were all dated to the latter half of the 6th century AD and represent the peak of ''haniwa'' expression.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 58.8 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 35 meters wide x 4.5 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 35 meter diameter x 5 meters high
===Other tumuli=== The other tumuli in the cluster are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with diameters of around 20 meters. They are well preserved and remain unexcavated.
The site is located a five-minute walk from the Yokoshiba Nakadai bus stop on the airport shuttle bus from [[:en:Keisei Narita Station|Keisei Narita Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/n411-019.html Chiba Prefecture official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/0000001838.html Yokoshibahikari town official home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.haniwakan.com Shibayama Haniwa Museum] {{in lang|ja}}
